title: Reddit Alleges Flawed Entry Won DeepMind/Kaggle AGI Prize
summary: A Reddit post presents evidence that the grand prize winner in the DeepMind/Kaggle &\#x27;Measuring Progress Toward AGI&\#x27; competition submitted a flawed and nonsensical methodology, questioning the integrity of the judging process. This situation raises serious concerns about research integrity and evaluation standards in high-profile AI competitions, potentially undermining trust in benchmark-driven progress claims. The post analyzes the winning submission&\#x27;s writeup, methodology, code, and data, alleging it generates random numbers and unfounded claims. The organizers have stated that the review was done properly and it is a matter of subjective interpretation.
context: The competition, sponsored by Google DeepMind and hosted on Kaggle, asked participants to design new cognitive-science-based AI benchmarks to measure progress toward AGI, with a $25,000 grand prize. The controversy highlights ongoing debates about how to evaluate AI systems and the rigor of peer review in competitions.

references.title: Measuring Progress Towards AGI: A Cognitive Framework
The ARC of Progress towards AGI: A Living Survey of Abstraction and Reasoning
